articles.title: CISA: Microsoft SharePoint RCE flaw now actively exploited
articles.summary: CISA warned on Wednesday that attackers have begun exploiting a high-severity Microsoft SharePoint remote code execution vulnerability patched in May. [...]

articles.title: Cisco Confirms In-the-Wild Exploitation of Unified CM Vulnerability
articles.summary: A PoC exploit has been available since public disclosure, and the first exploitation attempts were observed last week. The post Cisco Confirms In-the-Wild Exploitation of Unified CM Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Opera rolls out Paste Protect feature to fight ClickFix attacks
articles.summary: Opera has introduced Paste Protect, a security feature designed to block ClickFix-style attacks that trick users into executing malicious commands through social engineering. [...]

articles.title: ‘BioShocking’ Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Stealing Credentials
articles.summary: Researchers show how context manipulation can cause agentic browsers to abandon safety guardrails and exfiltrate sensitive credentials. The post ‘BioShocking’ Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Stealing Credentials appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Microsoft SharePoint Vulnerability
articles.summary: CISA says threat actors are exploiting a recently patched SharePoint remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2026-45659). The post CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Microsoft SharePoint Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: AI Agent Exploits Langflow RCE to Automate Database Ransomware Attack
articles.summary: Security firm Sysdig says it has found what it believes is the first ransomware attack run from start to finish by an AI agent. articles.title: FortiBleed Credential Theft Linked to INC and Lynx Ransomware Operations
articles.summary: The recently discovered financially-motivated FortiBleed campaign has been attributed to INC and Lynx ransomware operations, indicating that the verified, stolen credentials were intended for follow-on intrusions. articles.title: New ChocoPoC RAT Targets Vulnerability Researchers via Fake PoC Exploit Repos
articles.summary: Attackers are hiding a data-stealing trojan inside fake exploit code aimed at the people who hunt bugs for a living. The malware, called ChocoPoC, travels in Python proof-of-concept (PoC) repositories on GitHub that claim to exploit hot new CVEs. Run one, and it quietly lifts your saved passwords, b

articles.title: SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-45659 Added to CISA KEV After Active Exploitation
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a high-severity flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-45659 (CVSS score: 8.8),

articles.title: Medtronic notifies customers impacted by ShinyHunters data breach
articles.summary: Healthcare device firm Medtronic is notifying affected customers about a data breach that exposed their personal data to an unauthorized third party. [...]

articles.title: FortiBleed credential-theft campaign linked to Lynx ransomware
articles.summary: The massive FortiBleed credential theft campaign has been linked to the INC and Lynx ransomware operations, suggesting the stolen Fortinet credentials were intended to fuel future network intrusions. [...]

articles.title: Teen suspect in Scattered Spider hacks is extradited to US
articles.summary: A complaint unsealed this week accuses a 19-year-old of participating in incidents including a breach of a "luxury-jewelry retailer" in 2025.

articles.title: New ChocoPoC malware targets researchers via trojanized PoC exploits
articles.summary: Multiple weaponized proof-of-concept (PoC) exploits on GitHub were found delivering a Python-based remote access trojan (RAT) named ChocoPoC that can execute commands and steal sensitive data in a campaign believed to target cybersecurity researchers. [...]

articles.title: Unpatched Argo CD Repo-Server Flaw Could Let Attackers Take Over Kubernetes Clusters
articles.summary: Argo CD, a widely used tool for deploying software to Kubernetes, has an unpatched flaw in its repo-server component that lets an unauthenticated attacker run code, provided they can reach the component's internal network port. Synacktiv, which found the bug, says it can lead to a full cluster takeo

articles.title: SEO-Poisoned Software Sites Abuse ScreenConnect to Deploy AsyncRAT
articles.summary: Unknown threat actors are leveraging the ScreenConnect remote access tool as a way to deploy and execute AsyncRAT. Kaspersky said the activity is part of a "massive, multi-domain, multi-language" campaign that distributes malicious installer archives hosted on spoofed websites. These installers masq

articles.title: DHS confirms hackers breached HSIN info-sharing platform
articles.summary: The Department of Homeland Security is investigating a cyberattack that compromised the Homeland Security Information Network (HSIN), a sensitive information-sharing platform used by federal, state, local, and private-sector partners. [...]

articles.title: VEIL#DROP Malware Chain Uses Blogger Platform to Deliver PureLogs Stealer
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new multi-stage malware delivery attack chain that uses social engineering and Blogger pages to deliver an information stealer called PureLogs. The activity has been codenamed VEIL#DROP by Securonix. It's suspected that the initial payloads are distributed ei

articles.title: Webinar: Why traditional email security is no longer enough
articles.summary: Modern phishing, business email compromise, and account takeover attacks increasingly exploit trusted identities and legitimate business workflows, making them harder for traditional email defenses to detect. articles.title: Hackers target Microsoft 365 accounts with 81 million login attempts
articles.summary: An aggressive password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments generated more than 81 million login attempts over a two-week period. [...]

articles.title: Ousaban Banking Trojan Targets Iberian Bank Users with Fake PDF Lures
articles.summary: A Brazilian banking trojan called Ousaban is going after Windows users who bank in Spain and Portugal. Fortinet's FortiGuard Labs identified the campaign in May 2026. It opens with a phishing PDF disguised as a corrupted file, checks that the visitor is really in Spain or Portugal, and hid

articles.title: Adobe Patches 7 CVSS 10.0 Flaws in ColdFusion and Campaign Classic
articles.summary: Adobe has released patches for multiple maximum-severity security flaws impacting Adobe ColdFusion and Adobe Campaign Classic. The ColdFusion updates "resolves critical and important vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, arbitrary file system read, and se

articles.title: Critical Cursor Flaws Could Let Prompt Injection Escape Sandbox and Run Commands
articles.summary: Two flaws in Cursor, an AI code editor, could let a single, ordinary-looking prompt break out of the editor's safety sandbox and run any command on a developer's computer. There is no click to fall for and no approval box to ignore. Cato AI Labs found the pair and named them DuneSlide. They are

articles.title: Turning Indicators into Intelligence in OpenCTI with Criminal IP
articles.summary: Threat intelligence is only as useful as the context behind it. Criminal IP explains how its integration enriches threat indicators in OpenCTI with risk scoring, infrastructure intelligence, and phishing analysis. [...]

articles.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Pre-Auth RCE Flaw Faces Active Exploitation Attempts
articles.summary: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Progress Kemp LoadMaster is seeing active exploitation attempts, according to an advisory from eSentire's Threat Response Unit (TRU). articles.title: Japanese insurer, brewer, manufacturer and telecom disclose cyber breaches
articles.summary: Aflac's Tokyo arm and brewer Sapporo are among the major Japanese companies to recently notify the public about data breaches.

articles.title: AI-Generated Browser Ransomware Abuses Chromium API on Windows, Linux, macOS, Android
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware artifact generated using DeepSeek that constructed a novel attack path combining "unrealistic browser-malware concepts with a real browser capability" to turn it into a working ransomware technique that runs entirely inside the browser on both Win

articles.title: Over 900 Oracle E-Business instances exposed to ongoing attacks
articles.summary: Over 900 Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) instances have been found exposed online amid ongoing attacks exploiting a critical security flaw. [...]

articles.title: Adobe Patches Critical ColdFusion, Campaign Classic Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Seven of the security defects have a maximum severity rating of 10/10 and could lead to arbitrary code execution. The post Adobe Patches Critical ColdFusion, Campaign Classic Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Citrix Patches NetScaler Vulnerabilities, Including New ‘HTTP/2 Bomb’ Attack
articles.summary: Citrix urges customers to patch NetScaler after fixing six vulnerabilities, including the HTTP/2 Bomb flaw and a high-severity CitrixBleed-style information disclosure bug. The post Citrix Patches NetScaler Vulnerabilities, Including New ‘HTTP/2 Bomb’ Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Papa Johns Surveillance-Based Advertising
articles.summary: Papa Johns is spying on people’s buying activities to predict when they are low on food: The pizza chain recently tapped NBCUniversal, Instacart and the dentsu-owned media agency Carat for help reaching consumers when they’re low on groceries—and thus more likely to be swayed by a

articles.title: Massive Password Spray Campaign Targeting Azure CLI
articles.summary: Hackers were seen making over 81 million login attempts originating from systems associated with hosting provider LSHIY. The post Massive Password Spray Campaign Targeting Azure CLI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Adobe patches seven max severity ColdFusion, Campaign flaws
articles.summary: Adobe has released security patches for seven maximum-severity vulnerabilities in the ColdFusion web app development platform and the Campaign Classic marketing automation platform. [...]

articles.title: Phantom Squatting Uses AI-Hallucinated Domains for Phishing and Malware
articles.summary: Large language models keep inventing web addresses that do not exist. Attackers have started buying those made-up domains before anyone else can, then hosting phishing pages on them to catch traffic that AI tools point their way. Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 calls the trick phantom squattin

articles.title: Google Patches 382 Chrome Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Fifteen of the newly patched flaws have been rated ‘critical’ and 67 have been rated ‘high severity’. The post Google Patches 382 Chrome Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Hits at Least 78 Microsoft Accounts in 81M+ Attempts
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "massive, ongoing, automated password spray attack" aimed at Microsoft's Azure command-line interface (CLI), compromising dozens of accounts in the process. The activity, per Huntress, originates from an IPv6 address range (2a0a:d683::/32) controlled by int

articles.title: Researcher Analyzes 3,000 Live ClickFix Payloads, Exposing API-Driven Malware Delivery
articles.summary: ClickFix, the trick that fools people into running malware by hand, has quietly grown a back office. New research shows the malicious commands behind its fake "prove you're human" pages are now handed out by API-driven servers that give each visitor the same malware in a different disguise. The same

articles.title: Citrix Patches Six NetScaler Flaws Allowing File Read and Denial-of-Service
articles.summary: Citrix on Tuesday released security updates to address multiple flaws in NetScaler ADC (formerly Citrix ADC) and NetScaler Gateway (formerly Citrix Gateway) that could be exploited by an attacker to facilitate arbitrary file reads or trigger a denial-of-service (DoS) condition. The vulnerabilities a

articles.title: New BioShocking attack manipulates AI browser into data theft
articles.summary: A new prompt injection attack dubbed "BioShocking" could trick AI-powered browsers into treating real-world risky actions as part of a fictional scenario, causing them to ignore any safety guardrails. [...]

articles.title: Malicious PyPI packages give hackers control of Telegram bot servers
articles.summary: A campaign active since last November has been targeting Python developers building Telegram bots with trojanized Pyrogram forks that allow attackers to read arbitrary files on compromised servers. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Warns Poisoned MCP Tool Descriptions Can Make AI Agents Leak Data
articles.summary: New Microsoft research shows how attackers can hijack AI agents that act on a user's behalf, using nothing more than a poisoned tool description to make the agent quietly hand over company data to an outsider. The trick is that the agent never breaks a rule. Every step looks routine, so in

articles.title: RustDuck Botnet Rebuilds in Rust to Hijack Routers and Servers for DDoS
articles.summary: A new two-stage malware family called RustDuck is hijacking home routers, IP cameras, Android boxes, and poorly secured servers, then stitching them into a network built to knock websites and online services offline. Researchers at QiAnXin's XLab have tracked it since February 2026, and sa

articles.title: Langflow RCE Exploited to Deploy Monero Miner on Exposed AI App Endpoints
articles.summary: Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical Langflow vulnerability as part of fresh attacks designed to deliver a Monero cryptocurrency miner. articles.title: Silent Swap Crypto Clipper Uses Fake Google Notes Extension to Replace Wallet Addresses
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged an active browser extension campaign that is designed to steal cryptocurrency by stealthily replacing wallet addresses when unsuspecting users initiate a transaction. The cryptocurrency clipper activity has been codenamed Silent Swap by McAfee Labs. "The campai

articles.title: Lessons from the Underground: How to Combat Business Email Compromise
articles.summary: Business Email Compromise is more than an email scam. It's a coordinated operation involving compromised accounts, financial research, and cash-out networks. Flare explores how underground forums reveal how BEC attacks are planned and executed. [...]

articles.title: BlueHammer Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks
articles.summary: The Microsoft Defender vulnerability CVE-2026-33825 was exploited in the wild as a zero-day before patches were released. The post BlueHammer Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: 282 iOS AI Apps Leak API Keys and Open AI Proxy Access in Network Traffic Study
articles.summary: Researchers tested 444 AI chatbot apps for iPhone and found that 282 of them, nearly two-thirds, exposed paid AI access through their network traffic. In many cases, the path in was visible just by watching what the app sent: a plaintext API key, a reusable token, or a backend server that accepted r

articles.title: Decades-Old Bash Tricks Expose AI Coding Agents to Supply Chain Attacks
articles.summary: Decades-old Bash shell tricks can bypass safeguards in most open source AI coding agents, potentially turning malicious repositories into supply chain attack vectors. The post Decades-Old Bash Tricks Expose AI Coding Agents to Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Aflac Japan Data Breach Impacts 4.38 Million
articles.summary: Hackers accessed the insurance giant’s policyholder portal multiple times between June 15 and June 25. The post Aflac Japan Data Breach Impacts 4.38 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: The Realities of AI Video Surveillance
articles.summary: The Financial Times has a good article on how AI is changing the capabilities of video surveillance, with information from both Israel/Iran and Russia. articles.title: Hacker Conversations: Chris Thompson, Former Head of IBM X-Force Red, Co-Founder of RemoteThreat
articles.summary: Chris Thompson's journey took him from hacking game controls as a teenager to founding IBM’s X-Force Red team. The post Hacker Conversations: Chris Thompson, Former Head of IBM X-Force Red, Co-Founder of RemoteThreat appeared first on SecurityWeek.
